ocr.text: The data table presented provides a comprehensive overview of the various types of permits for advanced treatment
technologies employed in waterworks from 2007 to 2012. This period marks a significant evolution in the management of
treated water, particularly as municipalities increasingly adopted innovative treatment methods to address growing
environmental and public health concerns.
Type of Permit for Advanced Treatment  Permits (n) 2007–2012  Active Waterworks 2012  Treated Water (million m3/year)  Examples of Treatment Methods
Major components  43  29  13.26  addition of sodium hydroxide, precipitation by adding iron(II), flocculation with polyaluminium chloride
Inorganic trace elements  36  31  3.67  addition of iron chloride, iron sulphate or adsorption filters with iron oxide
Organic micro-pollutants  17  8  10.38  activated carbon filters, intense stripping
Microbiology  22  6  23.32  UV disinfection
Year  Tallmon et al. (2008)  Nb/Nc
Gene sets  All genes  Only TATA-box  Only TC[-39,-26]-PLMs  Only TATAΔ-PLMs  [-39,-26]-PLM-less
Percentage  Intron-less  18.8  24.5 (4e-9)  16.7 (NS)  17.5 (NS)  17.1 (NS)
Notably, the column "Treated Water (million m³/year)" highlights the substantial volumes of water processed, reflecting the

critical role of advanced treatment in ensuring safe drinking water supplies. 

The "Active Waterworks 2012" column further
emphasizes the operational landscape of these facilities, indicating how many plants were actively utilizing advanced treatment
methodologies at that time. Examples of treatment methods, such as the addition of sodium hydroxide and precipitation through
iron(II) compounds, demonstrate the diverse approaches taken to enhance water quality. Additionally, the inclusion of
flocculation with polyaluminium chloride illustrates the variety of techniques applied to optimize treatment efficiency. As the
data indicate, the effectiveness of these methods is crucial, with metrics such as Nb/Nc ratios and specific gene sets providing
insights into the biological implications of treatment processes. Together, these elements underscore the importance of
regulatory frameworks in guiding the deployment of advanced treatment technologies for sustainable water management.
